reviews.review_title: A very well designed, easy to use, app.
reviews.review_description: I used the app for a 360 tour, of a house my sister builds, so she can best feel what it will be inside. With bonus future like adding, sounds and music to each room, easy controls, and ease of use, my sister, who never uses VR, felt like home, and started to use it like a pro. Well done.

Working on a kitchen extension for our home and discovered Sentio...amazing. We used someone from Fiver to turn our plans into a sketchup model then uploaded to Sentio. Able to view the kitchen extension as if we were actually there and work out best place for oven etc and best layout of tables chairs and furniture as well as 'flow' of the space. Impossible to say how useful this is until you try it!

I've used the previous version of this app on my Gear VR. The new version is a great evolution, with simpler and intuitive interface and stereo 3D. Works very well and smoothly with the Quest 2. Great customer service too!

In terms of pricing, the available plans seem quite reasonable for professional use (designers, architects, real estate businesses).

That said, for a casual, non-professional user, who is only interested in having fun with simple personal projects from time to time, a monthly subscription fee can be a bit too much. I just wish there was an option for non-professional/personal use, with a regular one-time down payment like most other apps in the Oculus store.

Looking forward for that "leisure" version!

developer_response.body: Hi Mike! Thank you for this feedback. We have always tried to ensure our software is available to as many people in the industry as possible - including people who use it on a personal basis. We are going to incorporate this feedback for future iterations and pricing & keep helping individuals like you to the best of our capabilities with great support.

reviews.review_title: The easiest and fastest VR for sketches
reviews.review_description:
As architect and geek, frankly I've tested all new softwares and SentioVR is the easiest and fastest to present your 3D sketches in VR.
Above all, it's the most intuitive navigation. And for customers, navigating in VR is not necessarily easy, especially for neophytes. This is the simplest of those that I've been able to test.
I therefore give 5 stars because it keeps its promises, but it is not yet flawless. You have to understand 5 stars for sketch presentations (otherwise only 4 for architecture), for very consistent 3D renderings, it's not 100% ready yet. If your 3D is too heavy then you risk having errors. For rendering, wired solutions naturally still offer better results, and these solutions should therefore be preferred. But to navigate quickly and easily in your VR and wirelessly it's really what I have found best! Perfect for a quick presentation, not too textured, with the quality in this case more than enough.

Special thanks to the support service, it give you advise and quick answers!

Sentio VR allows quite simply to convert any SketchUp plans into embedded house or objets.

This allows me to visit my future home and see volumes in an jour (plans sends by architect).

Advice : walk and use arms to measure volumes, joystick locomotion made the volumes smaller than Real. I should have try outside to walk freely

I'd like to keep the project i just try in trial version.

Would tout consider direct mode from pc ?

reviews.review_title: Bridging a needed gap and super helpful
reviews.review_description: I bought this app with the goal of transferring a house plan I had created in SketchUp to my Oculus headset. Like any new tech, it took a little learning, but it worked. And it was awesome. The ability to walk around and through a house we designed in SketchUp on the headset was just awesome. $59/month was cheap to get that done in my opinion. Also the founder Ravi was incredibly helpful in following up and making sure it was working well for me. Thank you!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review. We are glad your experience was nice 🙂

reviews.review_description: i'm very pleased to have discovered Sentio VR.  As an architect and former professor of industrial design, i have always had an interest in technology and design, and as a SketchUp user for seven years for personal projects, i see Sentio VR as an important add-on to the tools of design research.  i am working on an experimental house, with complex geometry, where visualizing spaces and connections is not really obvious even to a trained eye in ordinary drawings.  Being able to look around in 3D, and experience the building 'in person' is crucial to understanding it, not only spatially, but also the connections, details and materials, which can be studied up close.  even with my Oculus Go, which is not as powerful as the recommended Quest 2, I have been able to learn, and see, a great deal about the projects i am working on.  i have tried other sketchup VR plugins with less success, i.e. not getting scale right, resolution which is not as clear, confusing interface, etc.  i would offer praise to the company and thanks to Kriti for her kindness and intelligence in getting me set up with an account.   i look forward to exploring the VR capabilities further, and would strongly recommend this app to students and independent architects and designers, and encourage the company to make the app available affordably beyond the scope of bigger architectural firms.

reviews.review_title: Easy to use & provided valuable insight
reviews.review_description:
I designed my next apartment floorplan in SketchUp and used SentioVR to view the space.

Getting my SketchUp project synced and live to open on my Meta Quest 2 standalone was very easy, you just need to install their extension plugin for your CAD app of choice. 

It helped me better visualize the space and easily share that experience with others.

reviews.review_title: Easy to use and great for classrooms!
reviews.review_description: I used the Sentio app with a group of high school students taking a civil engineering class who designed models of houses in Autodesk Revit. The app was easy to install and get working with Revit. I was really impressed with how simple it was to set up and how easily the students were able to do it. It added a lot to their experience by being able to walk around their models in 3D, and some even used it to demo them at the STEM fair (one of them won the prize for best project!). On top of that the customer support team was great and incredibly responsive. I had a question when we were uploading the models to their accounts and Manish from the Sentio team replied in something like 10 minutes. For supporting classroom use where time is limited, we really appreciated that.

At the time I'm writing this Sentio VR is only rated at 3.8 stars, which I honestly think is very unfair and doesn't do it justice.

I use Sentio VR professionally, mostly for 3D kitchen plans that I have modeled in SketchUp, I then let my clients view their future bespoke kitchen through the Oculus which gives them so much more information about what it is they are buying, they can tell me if the handle positions are correct, the height of the worktop, the general layout with the appliances and so much more.

As someone who then has to manufacture the kitchen myself, it saves me so much time and money not having to revise drawings adding or removing things because the client knows exactly what they are getting, something they can't do fully with a traditional 2D plan.

I will continue to use Sentio VR and look forward to future development of the software.

developer_response.body: Thanks for this wonderful review and we are glad Sentio was useful for you to save time and money. Thanks to your review and others, our rating has moved up to 4.3. Cheers :)

reviews.review_title: Design home in my VR way!
reviews.review_description:
I have been structuring a 3D model in Sketchup for the refurbishment of my flat. The main reason is that I would like to turn the main bathroom into the “Jack and Jill” style. I can surely throw all the items into the model, but the question is that will the space still be comfortable for me to use? And how can I prove it?

Fortunately, I found SENTIO VR, a powerful VR app allows me walking into my 3D model to feel the actual space by myself – absolutely magic!! Will definitely recommend this great tool to my interior designer. Well done, SENTIO VR!
Sentio has been great for communication across a distributed team!

As an architectural BIM specialist I must say this product is the way to go!
I have researched and tested many different VR programs and this one is by far the most user friendly and cost effective. 

I was looking for a program to showcase 360 degree tours that would also host video and audio files. Sentio VR does it! You get more than just a wow factor. You get function and simplicity. 

Clients have no issues navigating tours using any smart phone, tablet, or smart TV.
I am very happy to support this team!

Augustine Martinez
HarrisDoyle Homes BIM Specialist

developer_response.body: Thanks, Harris. We are glad you rate us so highly & grateful to customers like you who help us build this "game-changing" software together. Cheers!

reviews.review_description: As a professional in the architecture industry, I can say that Sentio VR is one of a kind. Sentio is one of, if not the only, app for the Oculus Quest that offers architectural to VR capabilities. Sentio VR has plugins for both Revit and Sketchup, that allow you to upload your models directly from the native programs. Through their website, you are also able to upload 360 panoramas created in rendering software like Lumion. Everything in Sentio is cloud based, which gives us so much flexibility with where we can bring our headset to set up presentations for clients. The interface in both the Sentio App and their website is very clean, modern, and extremely easy to use. The Sentio team is very helpful, responsive, and genuinely takes your comments and suggestions into consideration. They whole-heartedly are committed to creating the best experience possible and building a software that changes the way we work in Architecture. For anyone considering getting into VR for Architecture, I would highly recommend Sentio VR.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing a detailed review and recommending Sentio VR to other architecture and design professionals. We appreciate the feedback from customers like you so that we can build the simplest immersive collaboration software for the AEC industry. Thanks!

reviews.review_description:
I just bought a house and used Sketchup to design it. I wanted to be able to walk through my model in VR, and then I found Sentiovr. 

When I first used it, I thought I might encounter problems in some ways, but Sentiovr totally blow my mind! It was very easy to use, extremely user-friendly! I spent less than five minutes to sign up, download, install and entering VR when I first used it. Everything is so smooth. 

I'm very happy with Sentiovr and recommend it to people around me, and I even teach friends who also want to decorate their houses on their own. They know nothing about Sketchup and VR and they learn fast! Great job Sentiovr team!

developer_response.body: Thank you for your wonderful review. We're glad Sentio was useful for you to walk through your future house without any issues!

reviews.review_title: Keep your client updated with progress
reviews.review_description: We've been using SENTIO VR for just a few months now. We are all very impressed with the results especially with the ability to show to our clients how their scheme design is developing in terms of both finishes and function. It is especially useful for clients that live and work on the other side of the world. I think it is excellent value for money.  The SENTIO team are sensational, extremely helpful and very patient with this old boomer!. God bless them for their patience.

Very nice and intuitive software, simple, easy to handle, no need to be a specialist, what makes it attractive is the possibility to load your projects once you 've finished editing your 360°Tours and creating your 3D Model and start touring via the App in your Oculus Quest Headset! so cool! Honestly there is much to do here when one knows what they want. The SENTIO Team is very helpful! Good job, keep improving.

We tested a few options for VR modeling, we found this to be the best one for us. First tried out the trial version, and now learning and taking every advantage possible from the Lite version, , so far we find it helpful and a very intuitive app, works great. We would love to see a mix between the photo-realistic quality from the 360 tours and the 360 walkthroughs, which I'm sure will be coming up in the next upgrades. We model in SketchUp and now testing 360 tours with Enscape renders,  Support/help team were very kind and helpful to answer and guide us through.

reviews.review_title: Useful for both 360° and 3D Model VTour
reviews.review_description: Very intuitive software, simple, easy to handle, no need to be a specialist, what makes it attractive is the possibility to load your projects once you 've finished editing your 360°Tours and creating your 3D Model and start touring via the App in your Oculus Quest Headset! so cool! An also very affordable. Honestly there is much to do Here when one knows what they want. Very Happy to have shared my experience with the SENTIO Team, Very Helpful informations from the support Team! Good Job Guys, Keep improving, Blessings.

Indeed, this product is surprising I have been using Google SketchUp for 8 years now and I have never felt such great satisfaction. 

I am passionate about architecture nothing is more incredible than being able to enter into your own creations and allow you to give a great vision to others

do not hesitate ! the software is really nice especially if you want to have a better view of your creations!

developer_response.body: Great that you were able to communicate your vision using SENTIO VR as an architect. Thank you for the nice review 🙂
